page: Amazon Web Services m7a.8xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m7a.8xlarge demonstrates strong performance, placing in the top 25% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests, as well as Geekbench and Passmark CPU suites. Memory performance is mixed, with read-only small block bandwidth scoring in the top 25%, while large block read-only bandwidth and Passmark memory scores fall into the middle 50% average tier. The system excels in cryptographic and database operations, as evidenced by strong Passmark encryption and database scores, alongside high Redis SET throughput of 67.43 million operations per second.

For specialized workloads, the server shows top-tier performance in LLM inference speeds for small 135M models, and strong performance for medium 7B model text generation. However, performance scales down to average for larger 70B models. Given its balanced resource allocation and dedicated CPU cores, this instance offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for general-purpose workloads. It is highly suitable for software compilation, database hosting, web serving, and moderate machine learning inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz and utilizing the Nitro hypervisor, this server delivers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate strong CPU and cryptographic performance, placing in the top 25% for multi-core processing, software compilation, and database operations. It also achieves top-tier results in small-scale LLM inference. This server is well-suited for database hosting, software development pipelines, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.8xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Nitro hypervisor virtualization, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC general-purpose instance delivering balanced DDR5 memory performance and strong multi-core processing capabilities.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
128 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Strong CPU, cryptographic, and database benchmark performance
Top-tier inference speeds for small-scale language models
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for database hosting, software compilation, and web serving
